questions.question: Bright orange giant that marks the eye of Taurus.
Famous red supergiant forming the shoulder of Orion.
Earthy, warm spice made from the dried seeds of a parsley-family plant, common in chili powder and curry.
Umami-rich Japanese broth made from kombu and katsuobushi.
Twice-yearly moment when day and night are nearly equal worldwide.
Dynamic marking indicating to play loudly.
Largest moon in the solar system, orbiting Jupiter.
Middle Eastern spread of mashed chickpeas, tahini, lemon, and garlic.

Region of icy bodies and dwarf planets beyond Neptune's orbit.
Instruction to play notes smoothly and connected.
Smallest and innermost planet of the solar system.
Expressive piano piece evocative of night, notably by Chopin.
Interval spanning eight diatonic scale degrees.
Rapidly spinning neutron star that emits regular radio bursts.
British term for an eighth-note value.

Costly spice of crimson crocus stigmas that turns dishes golden.

Levantine herb blend featuring thyme, sesame seeds, and sumac.
